lessons.text: You cannot escape action, even for a moment.
lessons.rationale: Renouncing deeds brings neither actionlessness nor perfection; Nature compels everyone to act ceaselessly.

lessons.text: Don't fake restraint while your mind chases pleasures.
lessons.rationale: Stilling the limbs while brooding on sense-objects is hypocrisy; true yoga controls the senses and still acts.

lessons.text: Do your appointed work; action beats inaction.
lessons.rationale: Perform your bounden duty, for even sustaining the body is impossible through inaction.

lessons.text: Perform every action as an offering, not for gain.
lessons.rationale: Work done for sacrifice frees you; all other action binds. Act for that sake alone, free of attachment.

lessons.text: Repay what sustains you; take nothing for free.
lessons.rationale: Mutual nourishment sustains creation; one who enjoys the gods' gifts without offering back is a thief, and who cooks for himself alone consumes sin.

lessons.text: Keep the wheel of giving turning, or live in vain.
lessons.rationale: Food, rain, sacrifice, and action form one turning wheel rooted in Brahma; whoever refuses to keep it moving lives pointlessly.

lessons.text: The self-fulfilled need nothing from action or others.
lessons.rationale: One who rejoices and rests wholly in the Self has nothing to gain from deeds and depends on no being.

lessons.text: Work without attachment, and you reach the Supreme.
lessons.rationale: Always do what must be done detached; by unattached action one attains the Supreme.

lessons.text: Set the standard; the world follows its leaders.
lessons.rationale: Janaka reached perfection through action; whatever a great person does and establishes, the world imitates.

lessons.text: Keep working though you need nothing, for the world's sake.
lessons.rationale: Krishna has nothing left to attain yet acts, because if he stopped, people would falter and the worlds would fall into ruin.

lessons.text: Work detached for others' good, without unsettling them.
lessons.rationale: The wise act unattached wishing the world's welfare, and must not disturb the minds of those still attached to action.

lessons.text: Nature does the work; drop 'I am the doer.'
lessons.rationale: Egoism alone claims doership; one who knows the gunas merely move among gunas stays unattached.

lessons.text: Offer all your actions to the Divine and fight.
lessons.rationale: Surrender every act to the Divine, mind fixed on the Self, free of hope, ego, and mental fever.

lessons.text: Practice this teaching with faith, never scorn.
lessons.rationale: Those who follow it with faith are freed from bondage; those who criticize and ignore it are lost.

lessons.text: Never let attraction and aversion rule you.
lessons.rationale: Even the wise follow their nature; attachment and aversion seated in the senses are enemies whose sway you must refuse.

lessons.text: Do your own duty poorly rather than another's well.
lessons.rationale: One's own duty, even flawed, surpasses another's done perfectly; another's path is fraught with fear.

lessons.text: Know desire and anger as your real enemy.
lessons.rationale: What drives a person to sin against his will is desire and anger, born of rajas, all-devouring foes.

lessons.text: Desire veils your wisdom like smoke hides fire.
lessons.rationale: Insatiable desire, seated in senses, mind, and intellect, envelops wisdom as smoke covers fire and dust a mirror.

lessons.text: Master your senses, then slay desire by the Self.
lessons.rationale: Control the senses first, then destroy desire; knowing the Self is highest, restrain the self by the Self and conquer this foe.

skipped.reason: Narrative: Arjuna questions why Krishna urges terrible action if knowledge is superior.
Narrative: Arjuna asks for one certain path to bliss; no distinct teaching.
Expository framing restating the twofold path of knowledge and action; covered practically by 3:4-3:9.
